{"action":"opened","number":53687,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/53687","id":1764467509,"node_id":"PR_kwDOABkWpM5pK6M1","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53687","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53687.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53687.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53687","number":53687,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"Switch LLVM codegen of Ptr{T} to an actual pointer type.","user":{"login":"maleadt","id":383068,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM4MzA2OA==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/383068?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt","html_url":"https://github.com/maleadt","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"This PR switches our code generation for `Ptr{T}` from `i64` to an actual LLVM pointer type (`ptr` when using opaque pointers, an untyped `i8*` otherwise). The main motivation is to simplify `llvmcall` usage (doing away with the `inttoptr`/`ptrtoint` conversions), and also make it possible to simply use `ccall` to call intrinsics with pointer arguments (where we currently always need `llvmcall` for converting to a pointer).

Changing codegen like this is a breaking change for `llvmcall` users, but we don't promise any stability there. Also, with the switch to LLVM 17 where typed pointers have been removed, all `llvmcall` snippets will have to be updated anyway, so this seems like a good time to make that change.

Before:

```llvm
julia> @code_llvm pointer([])
define ptr @julia_pointer_3880(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24) %"x::Array") #0 {
top:
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:65 within `cconvert`
   %0 = load ptr, ptr %"x::Array", align 8
; └
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:90 within `unsafe_convert`
; │┌ @ pointer.jl:30 within `convert`
    ret ptr %0
; └└
}
```

After:

```llvm
julia> @code_llvm pointer([])
define i64 @julia_pointer_1542(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24) %"x::Array") #0 {
top:
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:65 within `cconvert`
   %0 = load ptr, ptr %"x::Array", align 8
; └
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:90 within `unsafe_convert`
; │┌ @ pointer.jl:30 within `convert`
    %bitcast_coercion = ptrtoint ptr %0 to i64
    ret i64 %bitcast_coercion
; └└
}
```

This also simplifies "real code", e.g., when `ccall` converts an Array to a pointer. I don't expect that to affect performance LLVM codegen of Ptr{T} to an actual pointer type.","user":{"login":"maleadt","id":383068,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM4MzA2OA==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/383068?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt","html_url":"https://github.com/maleadt","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"This PR switches our code generation for `Ptr{T}` from `i64` to an actual LLVM pointer type (`ptr` when using opaque pointers, an untyped `i8*` otherwise). The main motivation is to simplify `llvmcall` usage (doing away with the `inttoptr`/`ptrtoint` conversions), and also make it possible to simply use `ccall` to call intrinsics with pointer arguments (where we currently always need `llvmcall` for converting to a pointer).

Changing codegen like this is a breaking change for `llvmcall` users, but we don't promise any stability there. Also, with the switch to LLVM 17 where typed pointers have been removed, all `llvmcall` snippets will have to be updated anyway, so this seems like a good time to make that change.

Before:

```llvm
julia> @code_llvm pointer([])
define ptr @julia_pointer_3880(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24) %"x::Array") #0 {
top:
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:65 within `cconvert`
   %0 = load ptr, ptr %"x::Array", align 8
; └
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:90 within `unsafe_convert`
; │┌ @ pointer.jl:30 within `convert`
    ret ptr %0
; └└
}
```

After:

```llvm
julia> @code_llvm pointer([])
define i64 @julia_pointer_1542(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24) %"x::Array") #0 {
top:
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:65 within `cconvert`
   %0 = load ptr, ptr %"x::Array", align 8
; └
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:90 within `unsafe_convert`
; │┌ @ pointer.jl:30 within `convert`
    %bitcast_coercion = ptrtoint ptr %0 to i64
    ret i64 %bitcast_coercion
; └└
}
```

This also simplifies "real code", e.g., when `ccall` converts an Array to a pointer. I don't expect that to affect performance type.","user":{"login":"maleadt","id":383068,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM4MzA2OA==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/383068?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt","html_url":"https://github.com/maleadt","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"This PR switches our code generation for `Ptr{T}` from `i64` to an actual LLVM pointer type (`ptr` when using opaque pointers, an untyped `i8*` otherwise). The main motivation is to simplify `llvmcall` usage (doing away with the `inttoptr`/`ptrtoint` conversions), and also make it possible to simply use `ccall` to call intrinsics with pointer arguments (where we currently always need `llvmcall` for converting to a pointer).

Changing codegen like this is a breaking change for `llvmcall` users, but we don't promise any stability there. Also, with the switch to LLVM 17 where typed pointers have been removed, all `llvmcall` snippets will have to be updated anyway, so this seems like a good time to make that change.

Before:

```llvm
julia> @code_llvm pointer([])
define ptr @julia_pointer_3880(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24) %"x::Array") #0 {
top:
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:65 within `cconvert`
   %0 = load ptr, ptr %"x::Array", align 8
; └
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:90 within `unsafe_convert`
; │┌ @ pointer.jl:30 within `convert`
    ret ptr %0
; └└
}
```

After:

```llvm
julia> @code_llvm pointer([])
define i64 @julia_pointer_1542(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24) %"x::Array") #0 {
top:
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:65 within `cconvert`
   %0 = load ptr, ptr %"x::Array", align 8
; └
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:90 within `unsafe_convert`
; │┌ @ pointer.jl:30 within `convert`
    %bitcast_coercion = ptrtoint ptr %0 to i64
    ret i64 %bitcast_coercion
; └└
}
```

This also simplifies "real code", e.g., when `ccall` converts an Array to a pointer. I don't expect that to affect performance In cases where the results of constant inference, like concrete-eval,
are used for union-split inlining, `isa`-blocks are generated using the
`result.edge.specTypes` stored within each `result`. However, it's been
found that the `edge` returned by abstract interpretation may have been
widened by the new `@nospecializeinfer`, which can result in invalid
union-splitting. To address this problem, this commit tweaks the
inlining algorithm so that it performs union-split inlining using the
original signatures that abstract interpretation used for union-split
inference, by using `match::MethodMatch`.

- fixes #53590","distinct":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/commits/d45581c18d649ad19313300d6766587d2aed0155"},{"sha":"f6504e4af93a3ce33391b2bd858c1a3e9fb9fe54","author":{"email":"6773d54b313bb67d007d6620b80c4c43e2db95a1@users.noreply.github.com","name":"Shuhei Kadowaki"},"message":"add `construct_[post]domtree(::[IRCode|CFG])` interfaces (#53638)

Fix that to fix #53521.","distinct":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/commits/321fb2ccda65aa122c3549ef4951c6890b440cf2"},{"sha":"4dcf35786ec164832e1eb961c9cdacae7974a27a","author":{"email":"7e3ed8186492aca8c039a0528716df31ad911f7e@juliacomputing.com","name":"Keno Fischer"},"message":"inference: Don't confuse frames in different interpreters (#53627)

Diffractor's abstract interpreter sometimes needs to do side queries
using the native interpreter. These are pushed onto the regular
inference callstack in anticipation of a future where compiler plugins
may want to recurse from the native interpreter back into the Diffractor
abstract interpreter. However, this introduced a subtle challenge: When
the native interpreter is looking at a frame that is currently on the
inference stack, it would treat them as the same, incorrectly merging
inference across the two abstract interpreters (which have different
semantics and may not be confused). The caches for the two abstract
interpreters were already different, so once things are inferred,
there's no problem (likely because things were already inferred on the
native interpreter), but if not, this could cause subtle and hard to
debug problems.","distinct":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/commits/4dcf35786ec164832e1eb961c9cdacae7974a27a"},{"sha":"a998082d9c78fad066f6ebfd250989f922976c5b","author":{"email":"ae2c7ca13760581dfcf54bbb5052dc9d615c487b@users.noreply.github.com","name":"DilumAluthgeBot"},"message":"🤖 [master] Bump the Pkg stdlib from 56c379045 to e0821116e (#53654)

Co-authored-by: Dilum Aluthge <dilum@aluthge.com>","distinct":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/commits/a998082d9c78fad066f6ebfd250989f922976c5b"},{"sha":"78351b5c478b65295dfa143d33ddc41f546baceb","author":{"email":"a764d401064aa588ac1cc0e11d482310d997c242@gmail.com","name":"Ian Butterworth"},"message":"Use Base parallel precompilation to build stdlibs (#53598)

Follow-on from https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53403

This extends `Base.Precompilation.precompilepkgs` to take a list of
configurations to precompile each package with, while parallelizing
across all packages and configurations, and uses it to build the stdlib
pkgimages.

It simplifies the stdlib pkgimage build process but is (currently)
dependent on having an accurately resolved Manifest.toml (Project.toml
included to make the manifest easier to make). Any new/removed stdlibs
or changes their dependencies will require updating the Manifest.toml.
It's a bit chicken and egg, but should be manageable with manual editing
of the Manifest.toml.

In terms of speed improvement:
MacOS aarch64 CI runner 6m19s before, 5m19 with this

Note that CI builds will show the basic print with timing of each
package, whereas local build will be the tidier fancy print without
timings.

Adding these methods lets `OffsetArrays` define
`getindex(::AbstractUnitRange, ::IdentityUnitRange)` without
ambiguities. This is in the domain of sanctioned type-piracy, as the
result is an offset range in general and cannot be represented correctly
using `Base` types.

Re: https://github.com/JuliaArrays/OffsetArrays.jl/pull/244

cc: @johnnychen94 

Edit: this also fixes an indexing bug in `IdentityUntiRange`:
master
```julia
julia> r = Base.IdentityUnitRange(-3:3)
Base.IdentityUnitRange(-3:3)

julia> r[2]
2

julia> r[big(2)]
-2
```

LLVM codegen of Ptr{T} to an actual pointer type.","user":{"login":"maleadt","id":383068,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM4MzA2OA==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/383068?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt","html_url":"https://github.com/maleadt","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"This PR switches our code generation for `Ptr{T}` from `i64` to an actual LLVM pointer type (`ptr` when using opaque pointers, an untyped `i8*` otherwise). The main motivation is to simplify `llvmcall` usage (doing away with the `inttoptr`/`ptrtoint` conversions), and also make it possible to simply use `ccall` to call intrinsics with pointer arguments (where we currently always need `llvmcall` for converting to a pointer).

Changing codegen like this is a breaking change for `llvmcall` users, but we don't promise any stability there. Also, with the switch to LLVM 17 where typed pointers have been removed, all `llvmcall` snippets will have to be updated anyway, so this seems like a good time to make that change.

Before:

```llvm
julia> @code_llvm pointer([])
define i64 @julia_pointer_1542(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24) %"x::Array") #0 {
top:
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:65 within `cconvert`
   %0 = load ptr, ptr %"x::Array", align 8
; └
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:90 within `unsafe_convert`
; │┌ @ pointer.jl:30 within `convert`
    %bitcast_coercion = ptrtoint ptr %0 to i64
    ret i64 %bitcast_coercion
; └└
}
```

After:

```llvm
julia> @code_llvm pointer([])
define ptr @julia_pointer_3880(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24) %"x::Array") #0 {
top:
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:65 within `cconvert`
   %0 = load ptr, ptr %"x::Array", align 8
; └
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:90 within `unsafe_convert`
; │┌ @ pointer.jl:30 within `convert`
    ret ptr %0
; └└
}
```

This also simplifies "real code", e.g., when `ccall` converts an Array to a pointer. I don't expect that to affect performance though.

There are a couple of other patterns that could be updated, e.g. the `type` argument to the `GCAllocBytes` intrinsic is currently still a This adds two functions: `waitany` and `waitall`, as discussed in the
issue #53226. These functions wait for multiple tasks at once. The
`waitany` function blocks until one task finishes. The `waitall`
function blocks until all tasks finish.

Co-authored-by: Shuhei Kadowaki <aviatesk@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Jameson Nash <vtjnash@gmail.com>","distinct":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/commits/8413b97c8063662665b8e3588b8a7ea85f7f4c34"},{"sha":"6f143eaf241f2f514bce550bf40bba0ed60711f9","author":{"email":"99e7f19e9f64df56d4e81dc00b8566e722cdd766@gmail.com","name":"Jameson Nash"},"message":"invert linetable representation (#52415)

Previously, our linetables (similar to LLVM) represented line
information as a linked list from callee via inlined_at up to the
original information. This requires many copies of this information to
be created. Instead we can take advantage of the necessary existence of
the line table from the child to flip this chain of information and
instead make each statement be a table describing (for each IR
instruction):
`(current line number, (index into edges, index into edges statements))`
plus a table of all edges, plus a table with the original line numbers
from the parser, plus the file name. This is all packed into the struct

    struct DebugInfo
        def::Union{Method,MethodInstance,Symbol}
        linetable::Union{Nothing,DebugInfo}
        edges::SimpleVector{DebugInfo}
        codelocs::String
    end

Which is described in doc/src/devdocs/ast.md for what each field means
and look at stacktraces.jl or compiler/ssair/show.jl to look at how to
decode and interpret this information.

For the sysimage, this saves several megabytes (about 113 MB -> 110 MB)
and about 5% of the stdlib pkgimages (294 MB -> 279 MB).

It also now happens to have the full type information for the inlined
functions. Now if you create an `IRShow.DILineInfoPrinter` with
`showtypes=true`, it can print that information when printing IR.

```
julia> @eval Base.IRShow DILineInfoPrinter(debuginfo, def) = DILineInfoPrinter(debuginfo, def, true)
DILineInfoPrinter (generic function with 2 methods)

julia> (@code_typed 1 + 1.0)[1]
CodeInfo(
    @ promotion.jl:425 within `+`
   ┌ invoke MethodInstance for promote(::Int64, ::Float64)
   │ @ promotion.jl:396 within `promote`
   │┌ invoke MethodInstance for Base._promote(::Int64, ::Float64)
   ││ @ promotion.jl:373 within `_promote`
   ││┌ invoke MethodInstance for convert(::Type{Float64}, ::Int64)
   │││ @ number.jl:7 within `convert`
   │││┌ invoke MethodInstance for Float64(::Int64)
   ││││ @ float.jl:221 within `Float64`
1 ─││││ %1 = Base.sitofp(Float64, x)::Float64
│  └└└└
│  ┌ invoke MethodInstance for +(::Float64, ::Float64)
│  │ @ float.jl:460 within `+`
│  │ %2 = Base.add_float(%1, y)::Float64
│  └
└──      return %2
)
```","distinct":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/commits/6f143eaf241f2f514bce550bf40bba0ed60711f9"},{"sha":"f9e08f7941efad238c13641cb72a0d383fd7eaba","author":{"email":"d5bb13125edd18131b05fea60dac664c2441eb0f@gmail.com","name":"Jeff Bezanson"},"message":"fix #52025, re-allow all implicit pointer casts in cconvert for Array (#53659)

fix #52025","distinct":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/commits/f9e08f7941efad238c13641cb72a0d383fd7eaba"},{"sha":"5fc16621822398419a45631a1c0fc421a5cbd88c","author":{"email":"ae72534ab058d0e923451127a496caf2bbc19eae@users.noreply.github.com","name":"Mosè Giordano"},"message":"Fix warning about comparison of integer expressions of different signedness (#53658)","distinct":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/commits/5fc16621822398419a45631a1c0fc421a5cbd88c"},{"sha":"a0cee554ee1ca40dfcc9449ede733e0af5916b76","author":{"email":"685de9ca20e58160a0d391940e22b87fb48d4502@gmail.com","name":"Oscar Smith"},"message":"add check for invalid state in `_growend!` slow-path (#53513)

This only triggers in cases where the user has done something pretty bad
(e.g. modified the size incorrectly, or calling `push!` from multiple
threads on the same vector without a lock). I'm very unsure if
`ConcurrencyViolationError` is the right error to throw here, but I
think most of the time, that is going to be the cause. This check is in
the slow path because adding extra checks here is basically free (since
it will run rarely, and will be batched with O(n) work to copy
everything over).","distinct":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/commits/a0cee554ee1ca40dfcc9449ede733e0af5916b76"},{"sha":"dcfad21f3c72cf7460eef1912039c103fcbebd7a","author":{"email":"1d7856cfed7ea7ab44057aebdfc171fe9c103401@users.noreply.github.com","name":"Kenta Murata"},"message":"Add throw option in wait(::Task) (#53685)

As we discussed with @vtjnash in PR #53341, it might be useful to
introduce the `throw` option in the `wait` function for `Task`. If
`throw=false` is specified, `wait` behaves like `_wait`; it prevents
throwing a `TaskFailedException`.","distinct":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/commits/dcfad21f3c72cf7460eef1912039c103fcbebd7a"},{"sha":"dcd1fb2148dd7affee9449e304a9b42e7feba1ce","author":{"email":"9724b4e6cd549fe9b1c21996f3f0108658b44517@gmail.com","name":"Jishnu Bhattacharya"},"message":"LAPACK: validate input parameters to throw informative errors (#53631)

This PR validates the input parameters to the Julia LAPACK wrappers, so
that the error messages are more informative.
On nightly
```julia
julia> using LinearAlgebra

julia> LAPACK.geev!('X', 'X', rand(2,2))
 ** On entry to DGEEV  parameter number  1 had an illegal value
ERROR: ArgumentError: invalid argument #1 to LAPACK call
```
This PR
```julia
julia> using LinearAlgebra

julia> LAPACK.geev!('X', 'X', rand(2,2))
ERROR: ArgumentError: argument #1: jobvl must be one of ('N', 'V'), but 'X' was passed
```

Secondly, moved certain allocations (e.g. in `geevx`) below the
validation checks, so that these only happen for valid parameter values.

PR switches our code generation for `Ptr{T}` from `i64` to an actual LLVM pointer type (`ptr` when using opaque pointers, an untyped `i8*` otherwise). The main motivation is to simplify `llvmcall` usage (doing away with the `inttoptr`/`ptrtoint` conversions), and also make it possible to simply use `ccall` to call intrinsics with pointer arguments (where we currently always need `llvmcall` for converting to a pointer).

Changing codegen like this is a breaking change for `llvmcall` users, but we don't promise any stability there. Also, with the switch to LLVM 17 where typed pointers have been removed, all `llvmcall` snippets will have to be updated anyway, so this seems like a good time to make that change.

Before:

```llvm
julia> @code_llvm pointer([])
define i64 @julia_pointer_1542(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24) %"x::Array") #0 {
top:
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:65 within `cconvert`
   %0 = load ptr, ptr %"x::Array", align 8
; └
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:90 within `unsafe_convert`
; │┌ @ pointer.jl:30 within `convert`
    %bitcast_coercion = ptrtoint ptr %0 to i64
    ret i64 %bitcast_coercion
; └└
}
```

After:

```llvm
julia> @code_llvm pointer([])
define ptr @julia_pointer_3880(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24) %"x::Array") #0 {
top:
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:65 within `cconvert`
   %0 = load ptr, ptr %"x::Array", align 8
; └
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:90 within `unsafe_convert`
; │┌ @ pointer.jl:30 within `convert`
    ret ptr %0
; └└
}
```

This also simplifies "real code", e.g., when `ccall` converts an Array to a pointer. I don't expect that to affect performance though.

There are a couple of other patterns that could be updated, e.g. the `type` argument to the `GCAllocBytes` intrinsic is currently still a `T_size`.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53687/reactions","total_count":4,"+1":2,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":1,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":1,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53687/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1991884353","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53687#issuecomment-1991884353","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53687","id":1991884353,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM52ub5B","user":{"login":"maleadt","id":383068,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM4MzA2OA==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/383068?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt","html_url":"https://github.com/maleadt","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-03-12T15:11:50Z","updated_at":"2024-03-12T15:11:50Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"I can't reproduce these llvmpasses failures...

This was encountered in https://github.com/JuliaGPU/GPUCompiler.jl/pull/545, where GPUCompiler.jl uses the new `jl_method_lookup_by_tt` to look-up `Runtime.unbox_uint64(::Any)` (as part of the runtime library compilation). Currently that's not allowed:

```julia
julia> Base.method_instance(identity, (Int,))
MethodInstance for identity(::Any)

julia> Base.method_instance(identity, (Any,))
julia: /home/tim/Julia/src/julia/src/gf.c:1485: jl_mt_assoc_by_type: Assertion `tt->isdispatchtuple || tt->hasfreetypevars' failed.
[84712] signal 6 (-6): Aborted
```

We previously used `_findup` for that, which always allows such queries:

```julia
julia> Core.Compiler._findsup(Tuple{typeof(identity), Any}, nothing, Base.get_world_counter())
(Core.MethodMatch(Tuple{typeof(identity), Any}, svec(), identity(x) @ Base operators.jl:531, true), Core.Compiler.WorldRange(0x0000000000001d4a, 0xffffffffffffffff))
```","created_at":"2024-03-13T14:03:40Z","updated_at":"2024-03-13T14:03:40Z","closed_at":null,"merged_at":null,"merge_commit_sha":null,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"requested_reviewers":[{"login":"vtjnash","id":330950,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMzMDk1MA==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/330950?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash","html_url":"https://github.com/vtjnash","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false}],"requested_teams":[],"labels":[{"id":171886684,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxNzE4ODY2ODQ=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/labels/types%20and%20dispatch","name":"types release-1.11"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-03-13T14:03:40Z","updated_at":"2024-03-13T16:39:54Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/53723","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53723","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53723.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53723.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"Now that `jl_method_lookup(_by_tt)` is an exported utility, it can be used for looking up unspecialized method instances, so relax an assertion that prevents it from being used that way.

This was encountered in https://github.com/JuliaGPU/GPUCompiler.jl/pull/545, where GPUCompiler.jl uses the new `jl_method_lookup_by_tt` to look-up `Runtime.unbox_uint64(::Any)` (as part of the runtime library compilation). Currently that's not allowed:

```julia
julia> Base.method_instance(identity, (Int,))
MethodInstance for identity(::Any)

julia> Base.method_instance(identity, (Any,))
julia: /home/tim/Julia/src/julia/src/gf.c:1485: jl_mt_assoc_by_type: Assertion `tt->isdispatchtuple || tt->hasfreetypevars' failed.
[84712] signal 6 (-6): Aborted
```

We previously used `_findup` for that, which always allows such queries:

```julia
julia> Core.Compiler._findsup(Tuple{typeof(identity), Any}, nothing, Base.get_world_counter())
(Core.MethodMatch(Tuple{typeof(identity), Any}, svec(), identity(x) @ Base operators.jl:531, true), Core.Compiler.WorldRange(0x0000000000001d4a, 0xffffffffffffffff))
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53723/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53723/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1994928268","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53723#issuecomment-1994928268","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53723","id":1994928268,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM526DCM","user":{"login":"maleadt","id":383068,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM4MzA2OA==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/383068?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt","html_url":"https://github.com/maleadt","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-03-13T16:39:53Z","updated_at":"2024-03-13T16:39:53Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"> I think you might corrupt the mt->cache later in this function if this assert fails to hold? Not sure what I was thinking before here.","distinct":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/commits/ed1d6a03f9929c3c7942d5e90c7b48f9fde49c36"},{"sha":"2d244019d375cf47ed34159fdef557a02ab82bad","author":{"email":"a9bc71ad7e03aacaf9ffb421502a9c0e7aa7b223@gmail.com","name":"gbaraldi"},"message":"Don't double increment nrunning when starting threads","distinct":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/commits/2d244019d375cf47ed34159fdef557a02ab82bad"},{"sha":"a4783b0eefb6d0edbb08dc88497c64b316feed5e","author":{"email":"a9bc71ad7e03aacaf9ffb421502a9c0e7aa7b223@gmail.com","name":"gbaraldi"},"message":"Also don't increment it for gc threads","distinct":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/commits/a4783b0eefb6d0edbb08dc88497c64b316feed5e"},{"sha":"df28bf7c3df5e5f2e456e4cf6a39b62ffb3f2cbd","author":{"email":"99e7f19e9f64df56d4e81dc00b8566e722cdd766@gmail.com","name":"Jameson Nash"},"message":"use afoldl instead of tail recursion for tuples (#53665)

It is easy to accidentally call these functions (they are used by vcat,
which is syntax) with very long lists of values, causing inference to
crash and take a long time. The `afoldl` function can handle that very
well however, while naive recursion did not.

Fixes #53585","distinct":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/commits/df28bf7c3df5e5f2e456e4cf6a39b62ffb3f2cbd"},{"sha":"7613c6954fd2d9168f1c805d9212f961a4765a67","author":{"email":"00d7e96c6e658373237a2b839013279e1d215663@users.noreply.github.com","name":"Cameron Bieganek"},"message":"Make some improvements to the Scoped Values documentation. (#53628)

Fixes #53471.

One thing to note, I changed the signature in the `with` docstring from
this:

```julia
with(f, (var::ScopedValue{T} => val::T)...)
```

to this:

```julia
with(f, (var::ScopedValue{T} => val)...)
```

...since the original signature in the docstring was too strict. I also
added this sentence to the docstring:

```julia
`val` will be converted to type `T`.
```

I added a couple tests that verify the conversion behavior.","distinct":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/commits/7613c6954fd2d9168f1c805d9212f961a4765a67"},{"sha":"b1dd26ae7c9630c69bb5c7289706a569b9e9964a","author":{"email":"38285590d0afff27dd8d60acc6d441012ebfc54b@users.noreply.github.com","name":"Diogo Netto"},"message":"remove duplicate definition of is_anonfn_typename (#53711)

They seem to be doing the same thing. Centralize the definition in a
single place.","distinct":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/commits/b1dd26ae7c9630c69bb5c7289706a569b9e9964a"},{"sha":"9ae7eab873199a529e3052fba8c69c5a7573302f","author":{"email":"7e3ed8186492aca8c039a0528716df31ad911f7e@juliacomputing.com","name":"Keno Fischer"},"message":"Implement proper macro scope resolution for generator/for (#53674)

I'm not super familiar with this code, but it appears to me that these
need to be treated as equivalent to implicit let blocks for the purposes
of macro hygiene, so add appropriate logic to the macroexpander. Fixes
#53673.","distinct":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/commits/9ae7eab873199a529e3052fba8c69c5a7573302f"},{"sha":"8d31f334269c6553553cad4220215a03c6da7ab3","author":{"email":"4d34f6e57821093c36316ee32d74a2bce7a4b6c6@gmail.com","name":"Florian"},"message":"Move `isexecutable, isreadable, iswritable` to `filesystem.jl` (#53699)

This PR migrates the methods `isexecutable, isreadable, iswritable` from
`Sys` to `Base`, but also generates an alias in `Sys` for backwards
compatibility.

Furthermore, `iswriteable` is renamed to `iswritable` in order to match
the already existing `Base.iswritable` method.

Suggested in
https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53320#issuecomment-1989217973.","distinct":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/commits/8d31f334269c6553553cad4220215a03c6da7ab3"},{"sha":"3d34f11d4ec843bf97e1cca29ca84f5166c6c6c5","author":{"email":"329e29da7eb82e411422ea138c8f325aa7ff6190@qq.com","name":"N5N3"},"message":"typeintersect: fix potential free `TypeVar` caused by chained inner var. (#53675)

Noticed when working on 02f27c24929ab2561bcb21d3f58c1d313ae5d6e8. The
substitution and re-sorting of inner vars are incomplete on master. This
commit re-organized the code by:
1. Flatten the inner vars into a reversed list and handling them just
like vars in norm bindings.
2. Then perform a global re-sorting on all vars.
3. After that, the inner vars get frozen and dependent bounds are
refreshed.","distinct":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/commits/3d34f11d4ec843bf97e1cca29ca84f5166c6c6c5"},{"sha":"2e876fc7f665cd660aeb8f0c260523c1a1ca6d29","author":{"email":"6773d54b313bb67d007d6620b80c4c43e2db95a1@users.noreply.github.com","name":"Shuhei Kadowaki"},"message":"inlining: remove ineffective handling for unmatched params (#52092)

The deleted branch was added in #45062, although it had not been tested.

I tried the following diff to find cases optimized by that, but I just
found the handling proved to be in vain in all cases I tried.
```diff
diff --git a/base/compiler/ssair/inlining.jl b/base/compiler/ssair/inlining.jl
index 318b21b09b..7e42a65aa4 100644
--- a/base/compiler/ssair/inlining.jl
+++ b/base/compiler/ssair/inlining.jl
@@ -1473,6 +1473,14 @@ function compute_inlining_cases(@nospecialize(info::CallInfo), flag::UInt32, sig
         handle_any_const_result!(cases,
             result, match, argtypes, info, flag, state; allow_abstract=true, allow_typevars=true)
         fully_covered = handled_all_cases = match.fully_covers
+        if length(cases) == 1 && fully_covered

+        elseif length(cases) == 1
+            atype = argtypes_to_type(sig.argtypes)
+            if atype isa DataType && cases[1].sig isa DataType
+                println("second case: ", only_method)
+            end
+        end

PR switches our code generation for `Ptr{T}` from `i64` to an actual LLVM pointer type (`ptr` when using opaque pointers, an untyped `i8*` otherwise). The main motivation is to simplify `llvmcall` usage (doing away with the `inttoptr`/`ptrtoint` conversions), and also make it possible to simply use `ccall` to call intrinsics with pointer arguments (where we currently always need `llvmcall` for converting to a pointer).

Changing codegen like this is a breaking change for `llvmcall` users, but we don't promise any stability there. Also, with the switch to LLVM 17 where typed pointers have been removed, all `llvmcall` snippets will have to be updated anyway, so this seems like a good time to make that change.

Before:

```llvm
julia> @code_llvm pointer([])
define i64 @julia_pointer_1542(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24) %"x::Array") #0 {
top:
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:65 within `cconvert`
   %0 = load ptr, ptr %"x::Array", align 8
; └
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:90 within `unsafe_convert`
; │┌ @ pointer.jl:30 within `convert`
    %bitcast_coercion = ptrtoint ptr %0 to i64
    ret i64 %bitcast_coercion
; └└
}
```

After:

```llvm
julia> @code_llvm pointer([])
define ptr @julia_pointer_3880(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24) %"x::Array") #0 {
top:
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:65 within `cconvert`
   %0 = load ptr, ptr %"x::Array", align 8
; └
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:90 within `unsafe_convert`
; │┌ @ pointer.jl:30 within `convert`
    ret ptr %0
; └└
}
```

This also simplifies "real code", e.g., when `ccall` converts an Array to a pointer. I don't expect that to affect performance though.

There are a couple of other patterns that could be updated, e.g. the `type` argument to the `GCAllocBytes` intrinsic is currently still a PR switches our code generation for `Ptr{T}` from `i64` to an actual LLVM pointer type (`ptr` when using opaque pointers, an untyped `i8*` otherwise). The main motivation is to simplify `llvmcall` usage (doing away with the `inttoptr`/`ptrtoint` conversions), and also make it possible to simply use `ccall` to call intrinsics with pointer arguments (where we currently always need `llvmcall` for converting to a pointer).

Changing codegen like this is a breaking change for `llvmcall` users, but we don't promise any stability there. Also, with the switch to LLVM 17 where typed pointers have been removed, all `llvmcall` snippets will have to be updated anyway, so this seems like a good time to make that change.

Before:

```llvm
julia> @code_llvm pointer([])
define i64 @julia_pointer_1542(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24) %"x::Array") #0 {
top:
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:65 within `cconvert`
   %0 = load ptr, ptr %"x::Array", align 8
; └
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:90 within `unsafe_convert`
; │┌ @ pointer.jl:30 within `convert`
    %bitcast_coercion = ptrtoint ptr %0 to i64
    ret i64 %bitcast_coercion
; └└
}
```

After:

```llvm
julia> @code_llvm pointer([])
define ptr @julia_pointer_3880(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24) %"x::Array") #0 {
top:
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:65 within `cconvert`
   %0 = load ptr, ptr %"x::Array", align 8
; └
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:90 within `unsafe_convert`
; │┌ @ pointer.jl:30 within `convert`
    ret ptr %0
; └└
}
```

This also simplifies "real code", e.g., when `ccall` converts an Array to a pointer. I don't expect that to affect performance though.

There are a couple of other patterns that could be updated, e.g. the `type` argument to the `GCAllocBytes` intrinsic is currently still a release-1.11"}],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-03-13T14:03:40Z","updated_at":"2024-03-15T16:01:58Z","closed_at":"2024-03-15T16:01:57Z","author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/53723","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53723","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53723.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53723.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"Now that `jl_method_lookup(_by_tt)` is an exported utility, it can be used for looking up unspecialized method instances, so relax an assertion that prevents it from being used that way.

This was encountered in https://github.com/JuliaGPU/GPUCompiler.jl/pull/545, where GPUCompiler.jl uses the new `jl_method_lookup_by_tt` to look-up `Runtime.unbox_uint64(::Any)` (as part of the runtime library compilation). Currently that's not allowed:

```julia
julia> Base.method_instance(identity, (Int,))
MethodInstance for identity(::Any)

julia> Base.method_instance(identity, (Any,))
julia: /home/tim/Julia/src/julia/src/gf.c:1485: jl_mt_assoc_by_type: Assertion `tt->isdispatchtuple || tt->hasfreetypevars' failed.
[84712] signal 6 (-6): Aborted
```

We previously used `_findup` for that, which always allows such queries:

```julia
julia> Core.Compiler._findsup(Tuple{typeof(identity), Any}, nothing, Base.get_world_counter())
(Core.MethodMatch(Tuple{typeof(identity), Any}, svec(), identity(x) @ Base operators.jl:531, true), Core.Compiler.WorldRange(0x0000000000001d4a, 0xffffffffffffffff))
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53723/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53723/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1999973553","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53723#issuecomment-1999973553","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53723","id":1999973553,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM53NSyx","user":{"login":"maleadt","id":383068,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM4MzA2OA==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/383068?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt","html_url":"https://github.com/maleadt","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-03-15T16:01:58Z","updated_at":"2024-03-15T16:01:58Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"OK, I guess this is just the design of the cache used in there. That's fair, I can just use the slow `findsup`-based lookup when we're dealing with a non-`isdispatchtuple`.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1999973553/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

LLVM codegen of Ptr{T} to an actual pointer type.","user":{"login":"maleadt","id":383068,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM4MzA2OA==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/383068?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt","html_url":"https://github.com/maleadt","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":183231688,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxODMyMzE2ODg=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/labels/codegen","name":"codegen","color":"d4c5f9","default":false,"description":"Generation of LLVM IR and native code"},{"id":1056009044,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMDU2MDA5MDQ0","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/labels/minor%20change","name":"minor release"},{"id":1071716919,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMDcxNzE2OTE5","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/labels/needs%20pkgeval","name":"needs pkgeval","color":"dd526b","default":false,"description":"Tests for all registered packages should be run with this change"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":9,"created_at":"2024-03-10T14:34:28Z","updated_at":"2024-03-18T13:22:10Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":true,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/53687","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53687","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53687.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53687.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"This PR switches our code generation for `Ptr{T}` from `i64` to an actual LLVM pointer type (`ptr` when using opaque pointers, an untyped `i8*` otherwise). The main motivation is to simplify `llvmcall` usage (doing away with the `inttoptr`/`ptrtoint` conversions), and also make it possible to simply use `ccall` to call intrinsics with pointer arguments (where we currently always need `llvmcall` for converting to a pointer).

Changing codegen like this is a breaking change for `llvmcall` users, but we don't promise any stability there. Also, with the switch to LLVM 17 where typed pointers have been removed, all `llvmcall` snippets will have to be updated anyway, so this seems like a good time to make that change.

Before:

```llvm
julia> @code_llvm pointer([])
define i64 @julia_pointer_1542(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24) %"x::Array") #0 {
top:
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:65 within `cconvert`
   %0 = load ptr, ptr %"x::Array", align 8
; └
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:90 within `unsafe_convert`
; │┌ @ pointer.jl:30 within `convert`
    %bitcast_coercion = ptrtoint ptr %0 to i64
    ret i64 %bitcast_coercion
; └└
}
```

After:

```llvm
julia> @code_llvm pointer([])
define ptr @julia_pointer_3880(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24) %"x::Array") #0 {
top:
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:65 within `cconvert`
   %0 = load ptr, ptr %"x::Array", align 8
; └
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:90 within `unsafe_convert`
; │┌ @ pointer.jl:30 within `convert`
    ret ptr %0
; └└
}
```

This also simplifies "real code", e.g., when `ccall` converts an Array to a pointer. I don't expect that to affect performance though.

There are a couple of other patterns that could be updated, e.g. the `type` argument to the `GCAllocBytes` intrinsic is currently still a `T_size`.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53687/reactions","total_count":4,"+1":2,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":1,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":1,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53687/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2003893169","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53687#issuecomment-2003893169","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53687","id":2003893169,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM53cPux","user":{"login":"maleadt","id":383068,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM4MzA2OA==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/383068?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt","html_url":"https://github.com/maleadt","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-03-18T13:22:09Z","updated_at":"2024-03-18T13:22:09Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"Turns out that even on LLVM 17 from https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53070, the IR autoupgrader knows how to handle typed pointers:

```
julia> Base.libllvm_version
v"17.0.6"


julia> function foo(ptr::Ptr{Int8})
           Base.llvmcall(
               """%2 = inttoptr i64 %0 to i8*
                  %3 = load i8, i8* %2
                  ret i8 %3""", Int8, Tuple{Ptr{Int8}}, ptr)
       end

julia> code_llvm(foo, Tuple{Ptr{Int8}})
define i8 @julia_foo_564(i64 zeroext %"ptr::Ptr") #0 {
top:
  %0 = inttoptr i64 %"ptr::Ptr" to ptr
  %1 = load i8, ptr %0, align 1
  ret i8 %1
}


julia> function bar(ptr::Ptr{Int8})
           Base.llvmcall(
               ("""define i8 @entry(i64 %ptrval) #0 {
                   top:
                       %ptr = inttoptr i64 %ptrval to i8*
                       %val = load i8, i8* %ptr
                       ret i8 %val
                   }
                   attributes #0 = { alwaysinline }
                """, "entry"), Int8, Tuple{Ptr{Int8}}, ptr)
       end

julia> code_llvm(bar, Tuple{Ptr{Int8}})
define i8 @julia_bar_581(i64 zeroext %"ptr::Ptr") #0 {
top:
  %ptr.i = inttoptr i64 %"ptr::Ptr" to ptr
  %val.i = load i8, ptr %ptr.i, align 1
  ret i8 %val.i
}
```

... so it's probably too breaking to make `llvmcall` error out when using `i64` pointers after switching to `ptr`. I'll try to make `llvmcall` smart enough to handle this transparently.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2003893169/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

PR switches our code generation for `Ptr{T}` from `i64` to an actual LLVM pointer type (`ptr` when using opaque pointers, an untyped `i8*` otherwise). The main motivation is to simplify `llvmcall` usage (doing away with the `inttoptr`/`ptrtoint` conversions), and also make it possible to simply use `ccall` to call intrinsics with pointer arguments (where we currently always need `llvmcall` for converting to a pointer).

Changing codegen like this is a breaking change for `llvmcall` users, but we don't promise any stability there. Also, with the switch to LLVM 17 where typed pointers have been removed, all `llvmcall` snippets will have to be updated anyway, so this seems like a good time to make that change.

Before:

```llvm
julia> @code_llvm pointer([])
define i64 @julia_pointer_1542(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24) %"x::Array") #0 {
top:
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:65 within `cconvert`
   %0 = load ptr, ptr %"x::Array", align 8
; └
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:90 within `unsafe_convert`
; │┌ @ pointer.jl:30 within `convert`
    %bitcast_coercion = ptrtoint ptr %0 to i64
    ret i64 %bitcast_coercion
; └└
}
```

After:

```llvm
julia> @code_llvm pointer([])
define ptr @julia_pointer_3880(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24) %"x::Array") #0 {
top:
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:65 within `cconvert`
   %0 = load ptr, ptr %"x::Array", align 8
; └
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:90 within `unsafe_convert`
; │┌ @ pointer.jl:30 within `convert`
    ret ptr %0
; └└
}
```

This also simplifies "real code", e.g., when `ccall` converts an Array to a pointer. I don't expect that to affect performance though.

There are a couple of other patterns that could be updated, e.g. the `type` argument to the `GCAllocBytes` intrinsic is currently still a `T_size`.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53687/reactions","total_count":4,"+1":2,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":1,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":1,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53687/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2004575025","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53687#issuecomment-2004575025","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53687","id":2004575025,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM53e2Mx","user":{"login":"maleadt","id":383068,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM4MzA2OA==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/383068?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt","html_url":"https://github.com/maleadt","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-03-18T17:53:00Z","updated_at":"2024-03-18T17:53:00Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"Not PR switches our code generation for `Ptr{T}` from `i64` to an actual LLVM pointer type (`ptr` when using opaque pointers, an untyped `i8*` otherwise). The main motivation is to simplify `llvmcall` usage (doing away with the `inttoptr`/`ptrtoint` conversions), and also make it possible to simply use `ccall` to call intrinsics with pointer arguments (where we currently always need `llvmcall` for converting to a pointer).

Changing codegen like this is a breaking change for `llvmcall` users, but we don't promise any stability there. Also, with the switch to LLVM 17 where typed pointers have been removed, all `llvmcall` snippets will have to be updated anyway, so this seems like a good time to make that change.

Before:

```llvm
julia> @code_llvm pointer([])
define i64 @julia_pointer_1542(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24) %"x::Array") #0 {
top:
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:65 within `cconvert`
   %0 = load ptr, ptr %"x::Array", align 8
; └
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:90 within `unsafe_convert`
; │┌ @ pointer.jl:30 within `convert`
    %bitcast_coercion = ptrtoint ptr %0 to i64
    ret i64 %bitcast_coercion
; └└
}
```

After:

```llvm
julia> @code_llvm pointer([])
define ptr @julia_pointer_3880(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24) %"x::Array") #0 {
top:
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:65 within `cconvert`
   %0 = load ptr, ptr %"x::Array", align 8
; └
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:90 within `unsafe_convert`
; │┌ @ pointer.jl:30 within `convert`
    ret ptr %0
; └└
}
```

This also simplifies "real code", e.g., when `ccall` converts an Array to a pointer. I don't expect that to affect performance though.

There are a couple of other patterns that could be updated, e.g. the `type` argument to the `GCAllocBytes` intrinsic is currently still a `T_size`.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53687/reactions","total_count":4,"+1":2,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":1,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":1,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53687/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2006982611","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53687#issuecomment-2006982611","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53687","id":2006982611,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM53oB_T","user":{"login":"maleadt","id":383068,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM4MzA2OA==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/383068?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt","html_url":"https://github.com/maleadt","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-03-19T11:52:14Z","updated_at":"2024-03-19T11:52:14Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"I guess, but we don't have much `depwarn`-like functionality in codegen. I could do something one-off, of course...

---

Another remaining issue is a failed assertion with the following code (reduced from the `compileall` test):

```julia
foobar(ptr) = ptr + UInt(0)
code_llvm(foobar, Tuple{Ptr{T}} where {T})
```

This generates simply:

```
Core.CodeInfo(code=Array{Any, 1}(dims=(2,), mem=Memory{Any}(2, 0x7fffecb10680)[
  Expr(:call, Base.add_ptr, Core.Argument(n=2), 0x0000000000000000),
  Core.ReturnNode(val=SSAValue(1))]
```

The call to `Base.add_ptr` is handled here: https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/9df47f238c44a92ca0962efbc4c42173d0c5ce54/src/codegen.cpp#L6180-L6201

The result of `emit_call` there is of type `Ptr{T}`, while `expr_t` (as looked up in `ssavaluetypes`) is the UnionAll `Ptr{T} where T`. This results in `update_julia_type` aborting when it creates `cgval_t` that expects the value to exactly match the type: https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/9df47f238c44a92ca0962efbc4c42173d0c5ce54/src/codegen.cpp#L1882

@vtjnash What's the intended design here; should `emit_call` also have returned a UnionAll, or is this difference expected and should the `cgval_t` allow it?

FWIW, it didn't use to be a problem as everything was `UInt` before it reached `add_ptr`.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2006982611/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

So that it can be tested in isolation easier.","distinct":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/commits/8f76c69cdc3f85c7d29265dfe749f900094a01a3"},{"sha":"2775c9a0dc42436fb4c6013c3037eaad335a7bd3","author":{"email":"a87f6485e8aa8163af7a61acec9c8b75e685fcec@users.noreply.github.com","name":"Cody Tapscott"},"message":"Fix handling of virtual exit node in `PostDomTree` (#53739)

This is an alternative to https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53642

The `dom_edges()` for an exit block in the CFG are empty when computing
the PostDomTree so the loop below this may not actually run. In that
case, the right semidominator is the ancestor from the DFSTree, which is
the "virtual" -1 block.

This resolves half of the issue in
https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53613:
```julia
julia> let code = Any[
               # block 1
               GotoIfNot(Argument(2), 3),
               # block 2
               ReturnNode(Argument(3)),
               # block 3 (we should visit this block)
               Expr(:call, throw, "potential throw"),
               ReturnNode(), # unreachable
           ]
           ir = make_ircode(code; slottypes=Any[Any,Bool,Bool])
           visited = BitSet()
           @test !Core.Compiler.visit_conditional_successors(CC.LazyPostDomtree(ir), ir, #=bb=#1) do succ::Int
               push!(visited, succ)
               return false
           end
           @test 2 ∈ visited
           @test 3 ∈ visited
       end
Test Passed
```

This needs some tests (esp. since I don't think we have any DomTree
tests at all right now), but otherwise should be good to go.","distinct":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/commits/2775c9a0dc42436fb4c6013c3037eaad335a7bd3"},{"sha":"9df47f238c44a92ca0962efbc4c42173d0c5ce54","author":{"email":"6773d54b313bb67d007d6620b80c4c43e2db95a1@users.noreply.github.com","name":"Shuhei Kadowaki"},"message":"post-opt: add more test cases for `visit_conditional_successors` (#53642)

This commit fixes the first problem that was found while digging into
JuliaLang/julia#53613. It turns out that the post-domtree constructed
from regular `IRCode` doesn't work for visiting conditional successors
for post-opt analysis in cases like:
```julia
julia> let code = Any[
               # block 1
               GotoIfNot(Argument(2), 3),
               # block 2
               ReturnNode(Argument(3)),
               # block 3 (we should visit this block)
               Expr(:call, throw, "potential throw"),
               ReturnNode(), # unreachable
           ]
           ir = make_ircode(code; slottypes=Any[Any,Bool,Bool])
           visited = BitSet()
           @test !Core.Compiler.visit_conditional_successors(CC.LazyPostDomtree(ir), ir, #=bb=#1) do succ::Int
               push!(visited, succ)
               return false
           end
           @test 2 ∉ visited
           @test 3 ∈ visited
       end
Test Failed at REPL[14]:16
  Expression: 2 ∉ visited
   Evaluated: 2 ∉ BitSet([2])
```

This might mean that we need to fix on the `postdominates` end, but for
now, this commit tries to get around it by using the augmented post
domtree in `visit_conditional_successors`. Since the augmented post
domtree is enforced to have a single return, we can keep using the
current `postdominates` to fix the issue.

However, this commit isn't enough to fix the NeuralNetworkReachability
segfault as reported in #53613, and we need to tackle the second issue
reported there too

PR switches our code generation for `Ptr{T}` from `i64` to an actual LLVM pointer type (`ptr` when using opaque pointers, an untyped `i8*` otherwise). The main motivation is to simplify `llvmcall` usage (doing away with the `inttoptr`/`ptrtoint` conversions), and also make it possible to simply use `ccall` to call intrinsics with pointer arguments (where we currently always need `llvmcall` for converting to a pointer).

Changing codegen like this is a breaking change for `llvmcall` users, but we don't promise any stability there. Also, with the switch to LLVM 17 where typed pointers have been removed, all `llvmcall` snippets will have to be updated anyway, so this seems like a good time to make that change.

Before:

```llvm
julia> @code_llvm pointer([])
define i64 @julia_pointer_1542(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24) %"x::Array") #0 {
top:
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:65 within `cconvert`
   %0 = load ptr, ptr %"x::Array", align 8
; └
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:90 within `unsafe_convert`
; │┌ @ pointer.jl:30 within `convert`
    %bitcast_coercion = ptrtoint ptr %0 to i64
    ret i64 %bitcast_coercion
; └└
}
```

After:

```llvm
julia> @code_llvm pointer([])
define ptr @julia_pointer_3880(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24) %"x::Array") #0 {
top:
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:65 within `cconvert`
   %0 = load ptr, ptr %"x::Array", align 8
; └
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:90 within `unsafe_convert`
; │┌ @ pointer.jl:30 within `convert`
    ret ptr %0
; └└
}
```

This also simplifies "real code", e.g., when `ccall` converts an Array to a pointer. I don't expect that to affect performance though.

There are a couple of other patterns that could be updated, e.g. the `type` argument to the `GCAllocBytes` intrinsic is currently still a `T_size`.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53687/reactions","total_count":4,"+1":2,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":1,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":1,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53687/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2007228085","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53687#issuecomment-2007228085","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53687","id":2007228085,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM53o961","user":{"login":"maleadt","id":383068,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM4MzA2OA==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/383068?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt","html_url":"https://github.com/maleadt","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-03-19T13:47:49Z","updated_at":"2024-03-19T13:47:49Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"I improved the depwarn, so let's PkgEval again:

PR switches our code generation for `Ptr{T}` from `i64` to an actual LLVM pointer type (`ptr` when using opaque pointers, an untyped `i8*` otherwise). The main motivation is to simplify `llvmcall` usage (doing away with the `inttoptr`/`ptrtoint` conversions), and also make it possible to simply use `ccall` to call intrinsics with pointer arguments (where we currently always need `llvmcall` for converting to a pointer).

Changing codegen like this is a breaking change for `llvmcall` users, but we don't promise any stability there. Also, with the switch to LLVM 17 where typed pointers have been removed, all `llvmcall` snippets will have to be updated anyway, so this seems like a good time to make that change.

Before:

```llvm
julia> @code_llvm pointer([])
define i64 @julia_pointer_1542(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24) %"x::Array") #0 {
top:
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:65 within `cconvert`
   %0 = load ptr, ptr %"x::Array", align 8
; └
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:90 within `unsafe_convert`
; │┌ @ pointer.jl:30 within `convert`
    %bitcast_coercion = ptrtoint ptr %0 to i64
    ret i64 %bitcast_coercion
; └└
}
```

After:

```llvm
julia> @code_llvm pointer([])
define ptr @julia_pointer_3880(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24) %"x::Array") #0 {
top:
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:65 within `cconvert`
   %0 = load ptr, ptr %"x::Array", align 8
; └
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:90 within `unsafe_convert`
; │┌ @ pointer.jl:30 within `convert`
    ret ptr %0
; └└
}
```

This also simplifies "real code", e.g., when `ccall` converts an Array to a pointer. I don't expect that to affect performance though.

There are a couple of other patterns that could be updated, e.g. the `type` argument to the `GCAllocBytes` intrinsic is currently still a `T_size`.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53687/reactions","total_count":4,"+1":2,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":1,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":1,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53687/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2009332053","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53687#issuecomment-2009332053","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53687","id":2009332053,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM53w_lV","user":{"login":"maleadt","id":383068,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM4MzA2OA==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/383068?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt","html_url":"https://github.com/maleadt","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-03-20T11:17:09Z","updated_at":"2024-03-20T11:17:09Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"OK, this is starting to look good. CI failures are the same as on master.

The performance evaluation looks very good; I can't reproduce the regressions (most of which occur on sub-10 ns benchmarks), and the allocation reductions are real. I guess I guess the `inttoptr`/`ptrtoint` conversions *were* impeding some LLVM optimizations.

---

I also wanted to add a codegen optimization to avoid runtime calls when using non-concrete pointer types. As encountered in https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53687#issuecomment-2006982611, but now avoiding a runtime call:

```julia
julia> foo(ptr) = ptr + UInt(0)
julia> code_llvm(foo, Tuple{Ptr{T}} where {T})
; WARNING: This code may not match what actually runs.
; Function Signature: foo(Ptr{T}) where {T}
;  @ REPL[1]:1 within `foo`
define nonnull ptr @japi1_foo_447(ptr %"function::Core.Function", ptr noalias nocapture noundef readonly %"args::Any[]", i32 %"nargs::UInt32") #0 {
...
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:316 within `+`
   %.unbox = load ptr, ptr %0, align 8
   %ptls_field = getelementptr inbounds ptr, ptr %tls_pgcstack, i64 2
   %ptls_load = load ptr, ptr %ptls_field, align 8
   %ptr_box = call noalias nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(16) ptr @ijl_gc_pool_alloc_instrumented(ptr %ptls_load, i32 752, i32 16, i64 131785508750544) #5
   %ptr_box.tag_addr = getelementptr inbounds i64, ptr %ptr_box, i64 -1
   store atomic i64 131785508750544, ptr %ptr_box.tag_addr unordered, align 8
   store ptr %.unbox, ptr %ptr_box, align 8
   ret ptr %ptr_box
; └
}
```

... but with a realistic invocation the intrinsic is passed a UnionAll type, which fails the `jl_is_cpointer` check and falls back to the runtime intrinsic:

```julia
julia> a = Ptr[C_NULL]
julia> bar(a) = @inbounds a[1]+0
julia> @code_llvm bar(a)
; Function Signature: bar(Array{Ptr{T} where T, 1})
;  @ REPL[4]:1 within `bar`
define nonnull ptr @julia_bar_1276(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24) %"a::Array") #0 {
...
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:316 within `+`
   %2 = call ptr @jl_add_ptr(ptr nonnull %1, ptr nonnull @"jl_global#1286.jit")
   %frame.prev8 = load ptr, ptr %frame.prev, align 8
   store ptr %frame.prev8, ptr %tls_pgcstack, align 8
   ret ptr %2
; └
}
```

@vtjnash What's normally the way codegen deals with this?","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2009332053/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

PR switches our code generation for `Ptr{T}` from `i64` to an actual LLVM pointer type (`ptr` when using opaque pointers, an untyped `i8*` otherwise). The main motivation is to simplify `llvmcall` usage (doing away with the `inttoptr`/`ptrtoint` conversions), and also make it possible to simply use `ccall` to call intrinsics with pointer arguments (where we currently always need `llvmcall` for converting to a pointer).

Changing codegen like this is a breaking change for `llvmcall` users, but we don't promise any stability there. Also, with the switch to LLVM 17 where typed pointers have been removed, all `llvmcall` snippets will have to be updated anyway, so this seems like a good time to make that change.

Before:

```llvm
julia> @code_llvm pointer([])
define i64 @julia_pointer_1542(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24) %"x::Array") #0 {
top:
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:65 within `cconvert`
   %0 = load ptr, ptr %"x::Array", align 8
; └
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:90 within `unsafe_convert`
; │┌ @ pointer.jl:30 within `convert`
    %bitcast_coercion = ptrtoint ptr %0 to i64
    ret i64 %bitcast_coercion
; └└
}
```

After:

```llvm
julia> @code_llvm pointer([])
define ptr @julia_pointer_3880(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24) %"x::Array") #0 {
top:
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:65 within `cconvert`
   %0 = load ptr, ptr %"x::Array", align 8
; └
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:90 within `unsafe_convert`
; │┌ @ pointer.jl:30 within `convert`
    ret ptr %0
; └└
}
```

This also simplifies "real code", e.g., when `ccall` converts an Array to a pointer. I don't expect that to affect performance though.

We currently have
```
julia> @sprintf("%07f", -Inf)
"-000Inf"

julia> @sprintf("%07f", NaN)
"0000NaN"
```

With this PR
```
julia> @sprintf("%07f", -Inf)
"   -Inf"

julia> @sprintf("%07f", NaN)
"    NaN"
```

release"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":28,"created_at":"2024-03-10T14:34:28Z","updated_at":"2024-03-21T07:11:42Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/53687","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53687","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53687.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53687.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"This PR switches our code generation for `Ptr{T}` from `i64` to an actual LLVM pointer type (`ptr` when using opaque pointers, an untyped `i8*` otherwise). The main motivation is to simplify `llvmcall` usage (doing away with the `inttoptr`/`ptrtoint` conversions), and also make it possible to simply use `ccall` to call intrinsics with pointer arguments (where we currently always need `llvmcall` for converting to a pointer).

Changing codegen like this is a breaking change for `llvmcall` users, but we don't promise any stability there. Also, with the switch to LLVM 17 where typed pointers have been removed, all `llvmcall` snippets will have to be updated anyway, so this seems like a good time to make that change.

Before:

```llvm
julia> @code_llvm pointer([])
define i64 @julia_pointer_1542(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24) %"x::Array") #0 {
top:
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:65 within `cconvert`
   %0 = load ptr, ptr %"x::Array", align 8
; └
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:90 within `unsafe_convert`
; │┌ @ pointer.jl:30 within `convert`
    %bitcast_coercion = ptrtoint ptr %0 to i64
    ret i64 %bitcast_coercion
; └└
}
```

After:

```llvm
julia> @code_llvm pointer([])
define ptr @julia_pointer_3880(ptr noundef nonnull align 8 dereferenceable(24) %"x::Array") #0 {
top:
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:65 within `cconvert`
   %0 = load ptr, ptr %"x::Array", align 8
; └
; ┌ @ pointer.jl:90 within `unsafe_convert`
; │┌ @ pointer.jl:30 within `convert`
    ret ptr %0
; └└
}
```

This also simplifies "real code", e.g., when `ccall` converts an Array to a pointer. I don't expect that to affect performance though.

There are a couple of other patterns that could be updated, e.g. the `type` argument to the `GCAllocBytes` intrinsic is currently still a `T_size`.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53687/reactions","total_count":4,"+1":2,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":1,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":1,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53687/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2011353742","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53687#issuecomment-2011353742","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53687","id":2011353742,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM534tKO","user":{"login":"maleadt","id":383068,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM4MzA2OA==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/383068?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt","html_url":"https://github.com/maleadt","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maleadt/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-03-21T07:11:41Z","updated_at":"2024-03-21T07:11:41Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"And PkgEval looks good too: ExaTron failure is a AMDGPU.jl bug (filed downstream), Atomix.jl fails due to overly strict doctests stumbling over the depwarn, and the other failures are unrelated.
